title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy that likely deals with the implementation or regulation of artificial intelligence in the Small Business Administration.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act is a policy that puts a temporary hold on the development of artificial intelligence data centers. The exact details and goals of the act are not available, but it likely aims to address concerns related to AI data centers.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote the use of artificial intelligence in the financial sector. This policy likely seeks to create a supportive environment for the development and implementation of AI technology in financial services.

title: To amend the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act to direct the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a pilot program and develop voluntary disclosure standards relating to the use of artificial intelligence systems by private sector entities, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to amend the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act to create a pilot program for developing guidelines on the use of artificial intelligence systems by private companies.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act appears to be a policy document outlining guidelines and regulations for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in China, although more information is needed for a detailed summary. This act likely aims to establish standards, security measures, and possibly economic policies related to AI development and use within China.

title: To direct the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study on the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce in the United States, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This policy directs the Comptroller General to study how artificial intelligence affects employees in the US. The study's purpose is to gather information for making decisions about workforce impacts.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This policy document, the Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026, aims to regulate the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence. The specific details of the Act are not available, but it likely proposes laws and guidelines to mitigate AI's effects on the environment.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: This document outlines the guidelines for the "Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026", which focuses on the safe and controlled use of AI technology. It aims to establish regulations and measures to protect against potential misuse of AI systems, enhancing national defense capabilities while preventing potential risks.

title: A bill to require providers of certain artificial intelligence systems to implement child safety by design, parental settings, and independent audits, to prohibit child targeted advertising and the sale or sharing of children's personal information, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill aims to make sure that providers of certain AI systems put safety measures in place to protect children and prevent the targeting of children with advertising, as well as the sale or sharing of their personal information. It would also require regular audits to ensure these safety measures are being followed.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy document with no available description, but it likely deals with regulating or establishing rules for Artificial Intelligence models.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act aims to establish a framework for regulating the development and deployment of artificial intelligence to ensure public trust. The details of the policy are not available due to a lack of description, but its intended focus is on promoting the safe and trustworthy use of AI in the workforce.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to educate students in the US about artificial intelligence, its applications, and its implications. The goal is to prepare students for an AI-driven future.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act aims to prevent artificial intelligence systems from censoring certain content. The policy's exact details are unclear, but the title suggests it will restrict AI's ability to block or restrict access to information.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This policy document likely relates to assessing potential risks associated with the use of generative artificial intelligence technology to prevent or respond to acts of terrorism. The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act aims to identify and mitigate possible threats posed by AI in this context.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This document outlines a law or act related to the Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence. The content and purpose of the act are unclear without more information.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 is an act aimed at regulating the use of artificial intelligence in elections to prevent fraudulent activities. This act is likely to introduce new laws and guidelines for the development and deployment of AI systems in electoral processes.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a law aimed at establishing governance regulations specific to the Artificial Intelligence sector. It likely sets out guidelines and standards to oversee the development, deployment, and use of AI in various sectors.

title: To require reports regarding artificial intelligence-related job impacts, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This policy aims to require employers to report the impact of artificial intelligence on their workforce. It is intended for other purposes, but the exact nature of these purposes is not specified.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill proposes to introduce an excise tax on large-scale artificial intelligence activities that are considered systemically important, aiming to regulate the development of AI. The tax is intended to ensure the AI systems are designed and deployed responsibly.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This policy document outlines the Environmental Impacts of Artificial Intelligence Act for the year 2026, suggesting a focus on the effects of AI on the environment. The document aims to assess and address the potential harm caused by the growing use of AI technologies on the planet.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 aims to ensure the use of artificial intelligence in the military is secure and responsible. This act seeks to establish guidelines and oversight to prevent potential misuse of AI technology in military operations.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from misinformation and scams facilitated by AI technology. It likely outlines regulations to prevent the use of AI in deceptive practices and protect individuals from potential harm.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act is a policy aimed at safeguarding consumers from misleading AI practices. It seeks to hold individuals and companies accountable for using artificial intelligence in a way that deceives or harms consumers.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act is a proposed law that uses artificial intelligence to track and assess heat emergencies. This policy aims to provide a more efficient system for monitoring and responding to heat-related crises.

title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives with respect to the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries.
action: Placed on the House Calendar, Calendar No. 67.
summary: This policy document expresses the House of Representatives' opinion on the use of artificial intelligence in the financial and housing industries. It likely outlines guidelines or recommendations for the implementation of AI in these sectors.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act aims to support and advance artificial intelligence in small businesses. However, without further information, the specifics of how it will do this are unclear.
